channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump signs late-night executive order abolishing handful of federal advisory boards
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order late Wednesday, eliminating a handful of federal advisory committees like the Presidio Trust and the Inter-American Foundation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House will celebrate Black History Month as some government agencies skip after anti-DEI order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration will celebrate Black History Month at the White House on Thursday, preserving a tradition at the same time that President Donald Trump's executive order ending the federal government's diversity, equity and inclusion programs has disrupted its observance el
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump backs idea to send some DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: President Donald Trump says he likes the idea to give some of the savings from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump signs order aimed at ending federal benefits for some immigrants
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — President Donald Trump has signed an order aimed at ending federal benefits for people in the country illegally, the White House said Wednesday, his latest in a blizzard of moves to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ninth Circuit rejects Trump's bid to reinstate birthright citizenship order
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday denied the Trump administration's emergency request for relief from an injunction that blocks it from enforcing Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Justice Department official urges quick dismissal of criminal case against NYC mayor
item.description: The Justice Department’s second-in-command demanded that a judge dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams. The mayor, who faces conspiracy, wire fraud and bribery charges in a five-count indictment, declared, “I have not committed a crime.” Manhattan federal Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expected to sign executive order terminating all federal taxpayer benefits to illegal immigrants
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday is expected to sign an executive order to stop illegal immigrants from receiving federal taxpayer benefits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge homes in on DOGE staffer duties in privacy lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday looked to pinpoint the exact duties of Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffers during a hearing over whether to cut off their unfettered access to troves of sensitive personal data. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Bass points fingers when grilled on Africa trip amid botched wildfire response
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass, who has come under intense scrutiny for traveling to Africa last month amid a botched fire response, is deflecting blame on the controversial trip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senators ask Trump's Labor Department nominee where her allegiance would lie
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Members of a Senate committee grilled Labor Secretary-designate Lori Chavez-DeRemer on Wednesday about her past support of pro-union legislation, her position on raising the federal minimum wage and her willingness to disagree with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge to weigh arguments for dropping NYC Mayor Eric Adams' charges
item.description: Adams pleaded not guilty in September to allegations that he accepted at least $100K in illegal campaign contributions.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: US top diplomat Rubio visits the UAE after landmark talks with Russia over Ukraine war
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates, wrapping up an overseas trip that saw the highest-level outreach between the United States and Russia since Moscow launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Retail recreational marijuana, take two. Cannabis management leader speaks to rollout
item.description: On Tuesday, the Office of Cannabis Management started accepting applications for licenses to grow and sell marijuana — for a second time. Eric Taubel, interim director, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Health agencies lose staff members in key areas as Trump firings set in
item.description: As the dust settles from the first wave of firings at health agencies, here's how many people got cut and the impact of the roles that were lost.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: New Florida Attorney General sworn in, first priority illegal immigration
item.description: Florida’s new Attorney General has been sworn in and he's hitting the ground, running. After taking the oath of office, James Uthmeier stood with Gov. Ron DeSantis and the cabinet holding an emergency meeting to implement immigration enforcement measures.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, PIX 11

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
